latest.content:

Ascent Resources PLC
11 March 2026

THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("MAR"). UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE, THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
11 March 2026
Ascent Resources plc
("Ascent" or the "Company")
Update on Slovenia Disputes
Ascent Resources Plc (LON: AST), the onshore US focused oil and gas company, is pleased to provide a short update on the Company's legacy Slovenian claims, where recent developments continue to reinforce
Ascent Slovenia Limited's (
ASL) position ahead of expected near
‑
term decisions.
Domestic Disputes
The Slovenian court has fully rejected Geoenergo's challenge to the jurisdiction of the domestic Arbitration Tribunal which found in ASL's (a wholly owned subsidiary of the Company) favour in relation to ASL's claim for approximately €7.8 million in unpaid hydrocarbon production proceeds plus late interest, as announced on 3 July 2025. The court also noted that no other creditor contests ASL's claim in the Geoenergo insolvency estate. Further court decisions are expected in the coming weeks/months (first in relation to Ascent's court filing for recognition of the arbitral award, and second on Geoenergo's lawsuit for the invalidation of the arbitral award). Recovery remains subject to the conclusion of the administration process.
Also as announced on 30 October 2025, ASL continues to defend claims brought by Petrol Geo d.o.o. against the Joint Venture and remains engaged in seeking an amicable resolution of both parties' respective claims.
Energy Charter Treaty Dispute
The Company is aware of no delays to the ICSID process and continues to expect the Tribunal's award on the merits this month. A further update will be provided in due course.
Enquiries

28 January 2026
Ascent Resources plc
("Ascent" or the "Company")
Grant of Share Options / PDMR Notification
Ascent Resources Plc (LON: AST), the onshore US focused oil and gas company, announces that it has granted options over a total of 50,000,000 ordinary shares of £0.005 each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") to members of the senior management team, including 25,000,000 options to its Chief Executive Officer, under individual share option agreements.
The options have an exercise price of 0.65 pence per Ordinary Share and will vest in three approximately equal tranches on 1 July 2026, 1 July 2027 and 1 July 2028, subject to continued service (or accelerated vesting in the case of a good leaver cessation, including death). Each tranche has an exercise period of five years from its vesting date. The options are not subject to performance conditions beyond continued service. The grant is intended to align the interests of the senior management team with those of shareholders.
The grant made to Mr Patterson is in accordance with the terms of his service agreement dated 1 July 2025.
Notifications have been made in accordance with the requirements of the UK Market Abuse Regulation and are set out below.

14 January 2026
Ascent Resources plc
("Ascent" or the "Company")
Update on Energy Charter Treaty Claim
Ascent Resources Plc (LON: AST), the onshore US focused oil and gas company, is pleased to update investors in relation to the Company's ICSID registered Energy Charter Treaty claim against the Republic of Slovenia. Further to the Company's announcement on 23 September 2025, the Company has received an update from the International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes which confirms that the Arbitration Tribunal has made good progress with the draft award and are still in deliberation. The Company still expects that the Tribunal's award on merits will be issued towards the end of Q1 2026. Further updates will be announced as necessary.

latest.title: Option Agreement for exploitation in Utah
latest.content:
26 Nov 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 9724I
Ascent Resources PLC
26 November 2025
NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY IN THE UNITED STATES, CANADA, JAPAN, SOUTH AFRICA OR IN ANY OTHER JURISDICTION IN WHICH SUCH PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION WOULD BE UNLAWFUL. ANY FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THESE RESTRICTIONS MAY CONSTITUTE A VIOLATION OF APPLICABLE SECURITIES LAWS. PLEASE SEE THE SECTION ENTITLED "IMPORTANT INFORMATION" TOWARDS THE END OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT.
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("MAR"). UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE, THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
26 November 2025
Ascent Resources plc
("Ascent" or the "Company")
Option Agreement for the exploitation of critical mineral-rich brines in Utah
Ascent Resources Plc (LON: AST), the onshore US focused oil and gas company, is pleased to announce that it has, together with its partner American Helium, LLC ("
AH
"), entered into an option agreement ("
Option Agreement
") with Neometals Ltd (ASX: NMT) and its partner Omaha Value, LLC (together
"NMT/OMA"
).
The agreement grants NMT/OMA a 60-day exclusive option (extendable by mutual consent) to negotiate a definitive access and use licence over Ascent and AH's portfolio of existing oil and gas wells and leases in the Paradox Basin, Utah, for the purpose of exploring and potentially extracting lithium and potash from critical mineral-rich brines.
Key benefits of the proposed arrangement
·
Represents a new monetisation pathway for Ascent's large Paradox Basin acreage position with no upfront drilling or development cost to the Company;
·
Provides access to deep, artesian brines previously intersected during historic oil, gas and helium exploration;
·
Leverages the critical mineral status of lithium and potash, which may qualify for accelerated permitting and potential US federal funding;
·
Utilises existing wellbores and infrastructure, significantly reducing time and cost versus drilling new wells.
Financial terms of the Option Agreement
The following terms are gross and corresponding benefits to be shared between Ascent and AH pro-rata of their respective interests in the portfolio, i.e. Ascent 49% and American Helium 51%.
·
Exclusivity fee: US$50,000 (payable within five business days);
·
Option exercise fee (if exercised): US$50,000;
·
Permitting fee: US$1.9 million payable on grant of relevant mineral leases, and pro-rated if fewer wells are available;
·
Annual licence fee: US$200,000 (payable in arrears);
·
Royalty on future brine production: 2.5% of gross revenue, increasing to 3.5% if commercial extraction has not commenced within five years of mineral lease grant.
Strategic rationale
This transaction highlights the embedded multi-commodity potential of Ascent's Utah and Colorado acreage across hydrocarbons, helium, CO2 sequestration and now critical mineral-rich brines. By partnering with an experienced lithium-focused developer in Neometals and a well-resourced local partner in Omaha Value, Ascent can unlock additional value from its existing asset base at minimal cost and dilution to shareholders.
Next steps
NMT/OMA will now undertake technical, legal and commercial due diligence during the 60-day exclusivity period. The Company will update the market on material developments as appropriate.
There is no certainty that the option will be exercised or that binding definitive agreements will be entered into.
Enquiries

latest.title: Update on disputes out of Slovenian Joint Venture
latest.content:
7 Oct 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 3541C
Ascent Resources PLC
07 October 2025
NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY IN THE UNITED STATES, CANADA, JAPAN, SOUTH AFRICA OR IN ANY OTHER JURISDICTION IN WHICH SUCH PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION WOULD BE UNLAWFUL. ANY FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THESE RESTRICTIONS MAY CONSTITUTE A VIOLATION OF APPLICABLE SECURITIES LAWS. PLEASE SEE THE SECTION ENTITLED "IMPORTANT INFORMATION" TOWARDS THE END OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT.
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("MAR"). UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE, THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
7 October 2025
Ascent Resources plc
("Ascent" or the "Company")
Update on disputes arising out of Slovenian Joint Venture
Ascent Resources Plc (LON: AST), the onshore US focused oil and gas company, is pleased to update investors in relation to the Company's ongoing domestic disputes arising out of the insolvency of the Company's former Slovenian joint venture partner, Geoenergo d.o.o.
As previously announced on 3 July 2025, the Company's wholly owned subsidiary Ascent Slovenia Limited ("ASL")
received a binding domestic arbitration award confirming its entitlement to an increased amount totalling approximately €7.8 million in hydrocarbon production proceeds owed to ASL by Geoenergo from historic production prior to Geoenergo entering insolvency in January 2024. ASL has now filed a court application to make the award enforceable. This filing includes a request for the Administrator to immediately pay €56,825, as specified in the Tribunal's decision, which is expected to be paid in full ahead of completion of the insolvency process. Recovery of the c. €7.8 million plus other ancillary amounts owed remains subject to the conclusion of Geoenergo's administration and consequently ASL may ultimately recover less than the full amount.
Following Geoenergo's insolvency, ASL has been defending (on behalf of the joint venture) a claim by Petrol Geo d.o.o.
(a connected party to Geoenergo by virtue of their common, significant and controlling shareholder Petrol d.o.o.)
under the Service Agreement ("SA") for approximately €440,000 of claimed invoices covering March 2023 to April 2024, alleged to be unpaid by the joint venture. The Tribunal agreed with ASL and found that it had no valid jurisdiction over amounts claimed up to 28 November 2023 and determined that Petrol Geo is owed approximately €240,000 for the period 29 November 2023 to 19 April 2024. While the Company is surprised that the Tribunal asserted jurisdiction for the period after 19 January 2024-the date on which the administrator was appointed and unilaterally terminated the joint venture and which consequently caused the SA to terminate at the same time-ASL is exploring a possibility of filing an additional (post-insolvency) claim against its former JV partner, Geoenergo d.o.o., for ASL's share of hydrocarbon proceeds produced during 20 January 2024 to 19 April 2024 (the date the concession contract was terminated). Ascent calculates these amounts to be approximately €420,000 and such amounts should be payable in full from cash currently held on account by the administrator, which stood at approximately €1.633 million as of 30 June 2025.
In parallel, ASL initiated mediation against Petrol Geo under the Framework Build Operate Transfer Agreement (FBOTA) seeking reimbursement of approximately €66,000 in prepaid easement costs-benefits ASL will no longer receive and which have unjustly enriched Petrol Geo-and to require Petrol Geo to meet its contractual obligation under the FBOTA to acquire ASL's 75% interests in plant and infrastructure acquired by the joint venture after October 2013. The parties are in direct discussions exploring an amicable, holistic solution to these interrelated matters. ASL remains focused on a pragmatic outcome, which could include ASL's exit from any remaining interests in plant and property in Slovenia; however, there is no certainty that agreement will be reached. Further updates will be provided as appropriate.
Enquiries
Ascent Resources plc
Jean-Michel Doublet
info3@ascentresources.co.uk
Zeus, Nominated Adviser & Broker
James Joyce / James Bavister
0203 829 5000
Fortified Securities, Joint-Broker
Guy Wheatley
0203 411 7773
Shard Capital Partners LLP, Joint-Broker
Damon Heath
0207 186 9952

company_30s.what_they_do: Ascent Resources plc is an oil‑and‑gas exploration company that focuses on developing hydrocarbon projects in the United Kingdom, the United States (Utah) and Europe (Slovenia). They hold exploration licences, option agreements and are actively negotiating the exploitation of identified fields.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits at the intersection of two important energy markets: the U.S. shale boom and the European gas supply debate. Its Utah option could tap into a growing domestic gas supply, while its Slovenian assets sit in a region with high strategic importance for European energy security. The company’s assets therefore have a clear, tangible value to investors who understand the long‑term demand for hydrocarbons.
company_30s.current_state: Ascent is still in the exploration‑phase, with no proven reserves yet. It has been burning cash to fund drilling and legal work, but recent court victories and a new Utah option suggest a shift toward a more stable operational profile. The share price is trading at a few pence, well below any realistic valuation of its potential assets.

rns_narrative: Legal resolution is the dominant storyline: a July 2025 arbitration win in Slovenia, a 14 January 2026 Energy Charter Treaty claim update, and a 11 March 2026 court decision on the same dispute. These events signal that the company’s biggest uncertainty is likely being cleared.

Operational progress is another theme: the 26 November 2025 option agreement for Utah exploitation and the 27 June 2025 update on Utah operations indicate that the company is moving from paperwork to drilling plans.
Corporate housekeeping – board changes, a new CEO and AGM results – shows that management is keeping the company compliant and ready for the next funding round.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism waiting for the legal verdict
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A small but vocal group of traders is betting on a 500% jump once the Slovenian case is fully resolved and the Utah option is turned into production. They point to the recent arbitration win as proof that the market will react strongly.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most skeptics focus on the high cost of litigation and the risk that the court decision could still be overturned. They worry that the company may still need to raise more capital before any cash flow materialises.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from pure doubt to a cautious hopeful tone after the July 2025 arbitration victory, but engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there is no public buzz, which means any new positive news could trigger a surprise rally.

near_term_catalysts: The next Slovenian court decision is expected within the next 4–6 weeks. A favourable ruling would remove the biggest legal hurdle.
The Utah option agreement is slated to be finalized in the next quarter. Once the company signs the final drilling contract, volume could jump.
A quarterly earnings release in Q2 2026 will show whether the company can fund its drilling plans without another capital raise.
Any announcement of a new funding round or a strategic partnership would reduce the cash burn risk.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume (double the average) and a break above the 5‑pence support level. A positive court ruling or a drilling‑contract signature will be the clearest confirmation of a mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share has fallen more than 70% from its recent high, indicating early‑stage capitulation. The APEX score of 8.0 shows some compression, so the next move is likely to be a sharp bounce rather than a sideways drift.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock sits in a deep crash zone. If the Slovenian dispute is resolved favourably, the price could rebound to the 2025 mid‑level, offering roughly a 150–200 % upside. The company’s new Utah option and recent arbitration win provide tangible catalysts that can trigger mean reversion.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is a negative court ruling or a failure to secure the Utah drilling contract. If the company still needs a fresh capital raise, the share could stay depressed or even fall further. The worst realistic downside is a further 70 % drop if the company remains in a cash‑burn mode.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 150 % (double the current price). Potential downside: 70 % (current price). The asymmetry is roughly 2.1:1, making the trade attractive for a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when volume spikes to 2× the 30‑day average and the price breaks the 5‑pence support. Size the position so that the stop at 4 pence limits loss to 20 % of the trade. Use a trailing stop at 2 pence once the price moves up 10 pence to lock in gains.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the Slovenian court decision is announced or the Utah drilling contract is signed. If the price fails to move above 6 pence within 30 days of the decision, consider exiting the position.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy or is delisted, the trade becomes terminal. No further action is recommended beyond liquidating any exposure.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Ast.L sits at a 70‑plus‑percent crash; a favorable Slovenian court ruling or Utah drilling contract could unleash a 150–200 % rebound, making this a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian play.
